# UC-11.2.12 ¬∑ Workspace Marketplace App Review

## Description

New marketplace app installs can expose Drive/Gmail data; tracks installs and OAuth scopes.

## Value

New marketplace app installs can expose Drive/Gmail data; tracks installs and OAuth scopes.

## Implementation

Maintain allowlist of approved apps. Alert on new apps with sensitive scopes. Quarterly access review with app owners.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Token audit (`authorize`), admin audit (`ADD_APPLICATION`, `INSTALL_MARKETPLACE_APP`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Maintain allowlist of approved apps. Alert on new apps with sensitive scopes. Quarterly access review with app owners.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:token" event_name="authorize"
| stats values(scope) as scopes by app_name, actor.email
| mvexpand scopes limit=500
| search scopes="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive" OR scopes="*gmail*"
| sort app_name
```

Understanding this SPL

**Workspace Marketplace App Review** ‚Äî New marketplace app installs can expose Drive/Gmail data; tracks installs and OAuth scopes.

Documented **Data sources**: Token audit (`authorize`), admin audit (`ADD_APPLICATION`, `INSTALL_MARKETPLACE_APP`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gws; **sourcetype**: gws:token. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=gws, sourcetype="gws:token". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_name, actor.email** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Expands multivalue fields with `mvexpand` ‚Äî use `limit=` to cap row explosion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with Google Admin console audit and Google Workspace reports for the same org unit and time window.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (high-risk grants), Bar chart (apps by user count), Pie chart (scope categories).

## SPL

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:token" event_name="authorize"
| stats values(scope) as scopes by app_name, actor.email
| mvexpand scopes limit=500
| search scopes="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive" OR scopes="*gmail*"
| sort app_name
```

## Visualization

Table (high-risk grants), Bar chart (apps by user count), Pie chart (scope categories).
